name: Rice Pudding (Hess)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Prepare a 1-quart casserole dish with vegetable pan-coating.
3. Scald (heat) milk in a double boiler over simmering water until just hot, not boiling.
4. In a bowl, combine beaten eggs, sugar, salt, cinnamon, and vanilla extract. Mix well.
5. Pour the hot milk mixture slowly into the egg mixture, stirring constantly to avoid curdling.
6. Spread cooked rice evenly in the bottom of the casserole dish.
7. Scatter raisins over the rice.
8. Pour the milk-egg mixture carefully on top of the raisins and rice.
9. Place the casserole in a pan of hot water that almost covers it, ensuring the casserole is above the water level by about an inch.
10. Bake for 45 minutes or until set when tested with a knife at the edge. Remove from heat and let cool slightly before serving.
11. Serve warm or chilled as preferred.
Food Exchange: 1 1/2 STARCH/BREAD EXCHANGE + 1 LEAN MEAT EXCHANGE
CAL: 159
CHO: 25g
PRO: 8g
FAT: 3g
SOD: 221
Note: For low-sodium diets, omit salt when cooking rice and exclude salt from the recipe.
